reasoning_model_1: The model generates the content 'How can this report have so many errors?!' with complete accuracy, matching the transcript perfectly (0.0s - 2.5s). Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Emotion' (Angry), the audio demonstrates a clear shift in tone. The onset at 'How can' (0.0s - 0.5s) starts with a forceful attack, and the pitch is elevated throughout. There is a distinct emphasis and increased intensity on the phrase 'so many errors' (1.5s - 2.3s), effectively conveying frustration and anger. The fluency is high, with no mechanical artifacts or unnatural pauses.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately synthesizes the text 'How can this report have so many errors?!' without any omissions or mispronunciations. model_comparison: Both models performed exceptionally well on this task. Content accuracy was perfect for both T1 and T2. In terms of the core paralinguistic requirement (Angry emotion), both models successfully adopted a frustrated, high-arousal tone that matches the user's request. T1 and T2 both utilized volume dynamics and pitch modulation to express anger effectively. T1 had a slightly more pronounced emphasis on the end of the sentence, while T2 maintained a consistent aggressive tempo, but the difference in quality is negligible. Both are fully correct examples of the requested feature control.
